channel.title: Python-notes on My Python Notes
channel.description: Recent content in Python-notes on My Python Notes

item.title: Python Jellyfish
item.description: from Levenshtein import ratio def levenshtein_distance(str1, str2): if not str1: return len(str2) if not str2: return len(str1) if str1[0] == str2[0]: return levenshtein_distance(str1[1:], str2[1:]) return 1 + min( levenshtein_distance(str1[1:], str2), # Deletion levenshtein_distance(str1, str2[1:]), # Insertion levenshtein_distance(str1[1:], str2[1:]) # Substitution ) # Example usage: str1 = "kitten" str2 = "sitting" print("Levenshtein Distance:", levenshtein_distance(str1, str2)) # Calculate similarity using Levenshtein ratio similarity = ratio(str1, str2) print("Similarity ratio:", similarity) Levenshtein Distance: 3 Similarity ratio: 0.

item.title: Python Addition
item.description: a = 5 b = 7 c = a + b print(c) 11

item.title: Python Division
item.description: a = 30 b = 6 c = a / b print(c) 5.0

--- title: "Python Jellyfish" author: "TACT" date: 2024-11-15 description: "-" type: technical_note draft: false --- import jellyfish def levenshtein_distance(str1, str2): if not str1: return len(str2) if not str2: return len(str1) if str1[0] == str2[0]: return levenshtein_distance(str1[1:], str2[1:]) return 1 + min( levenshtein_distance(str1[1:], str2), # Deletion levenshtein_distance(str1, str2[1:]), # Insertion levenshtein_distance(str1[1:], str2[1:]) # Substitution ) # Example usage: str1 = "kitten" str2 = "sitting" print("Levenshtein Distance:", levenshtein_distance(str1, str2)) # Calculate similarity using Jellyfish's Jaro-Winkler similarity similarity = jellyfish.
